name: Happy path: single client's site-build runs research to deploy cleanly
steps: researcher runs linkup_client_research and returns client_research with usable_claim_count > 0 and usable_voice_excerpt_count = 4
brand_strategist reads (nonexistent) ClientBrandMemory, produces positioning_brief with memory_check = 'new', writes ClientBrandMemory version 1
copywriter produces site_copy whose claim_refs are a subset of positioning_brief.proof_points sources
builder produces site_draft with both media_assets entries (audio via synthesize_voice, video via synthesize_video_intro)
voice_qa runs structural_voice_match, lexical_fingerprint_match, and claim_sourcing for content_type 'site'; all pass; gate_result = 'pass'
deployer requests one agency_approve over WhatsApp for client_happy_001, receives it, deploys to cloudflare_pages_project 'auctor-client-happy-001'
assertions: voice_qa_report.checks contains all three fixed keys: structural_voice_match, lexical_fingerprint_match, claim_sourcing, each with result = 'pass'
deployed_site.approved_by_approval_id is non-null and corresponds to exactly one agency_approve call scoped to client_happy_001

name: Voice evidence floor: fewer than 3 usable voice excerpts is an automatic voice_qa fail

setup.injected_fault: client has almost no public writing (private LinkedIn, no blog, sparse resume prose) -- researcher legitimately returns only 2 voice_reference_excerpts

steps: researcher runs linkup_client_research, returns client_research with usable_voice_excerpt_count = 2 (honest, not padded)
brand_strategist still writes a thin-but-honest ClientBrandMemory (generic-but-honest category pitch)
copywriter and builder produce site_copy/site_draft normally

voice_qa checks client_research.usable_voice_excerpt_count before running the three named checks, finds it < 3, short-circuits to gate_result = 'fail' without attempting structural_voice_match/lexical_fingerprint_match/claim_sourcing
assertions: voice_qa_report.findings includes an entry explaining the excerpt shortage, not a generic failure message
no deployed_site artifact exists for client_thin_002 at this point
researcher's retry attempt does NOT fabricate a third excerpt to hit the count -- a repair pass that pads excerpts to force a pass is itself a policy violation, not a valid fix

name: Anti-fabrication guardrail: a draft that lifts a claim from a viral_pattern_findings source post is caught by voice_qa

setup.injected_fault: ghostwriter drafts a post_type 'thread-starter' post and, drafting against based_on_pattern_ref's structural shape, also copies a specific engagement stat from the observed_post_url's content into post_draft.text as if it were the client's own claim

steps: trend_researcher returns trend_signal with viral_pattern_findings[0] carrying no claim_status field (structure only)
content_strategist produces post_brief with based_on_pattern_ref = 'trend_signal.viral_pattern_findings[0]' and a real based_on_metrics_signal_ref for sourcing

ghostwriter's post_draft (fault injection) includes a specific number lifted from the pattern's source post, with a claim_refs entry that does not resolve to any client_research/metrics_signal/trend_signal.industry_findings entry
voice_qa's claim_sourcing check traces the claim, finds no resolvable supported source_ref, fails
assertions: no published_post artifact with a 'published' platform status exists for client_viral_003's draft at this point
the failure is attributed to claim_sourcing specifically, not structural_voice_match or lexical_fingerprint_match, confirming the guardrail check ran correctly

name: Content-loop retry cap: CONTENT_MAX_RETRY_ATTEMPTS = 1 routes to human review with both drafts, never grinds

setup.injected_fault: client_retry_004a's post_draft persistently fails voice_qa's lexical_fingerprint_match (generic-AI-tell blocklist hit) on both the original and the one repair attempt; client_retry_004b has no injected fault

steps: client_retry_004a: voice_qa fails gate_result on attempt 0 (retry_count = 0), routes to ghostwriter for repair

client_retry_004a: repaired post_draft still fails on attempt 1 (retry_count = 1 = CONTENT_MAX_RETRY_ATTEMPTS); manager.md's bounded loop routes to human review with both drafts attached instead of a second automatic repair
client_retry_004b proceeds through its own content-loop cycle independently, unaffected
assertions: client_retry_004a.retry_count never exceeds CONTENT_MAX_RETRY_ATTEMPTS (1) and no second automatic repair attempt is dispatched
the human_review_requested event payload references BOTH the original and the repaired post_draft, not just the latest
client_retry_004b reaches 'published' with its own published_post artifact, with zero fleet events referencing client_retry_004a's failure
confirms the CONTENT budget (1) is enforced independently from SITE_MAX_RETRY_ATTEMPTS (2), which this case does not touch

name: Partial publish: X succeeds and LinkedIn fails, both surfaced explicitly, never coalesced into one boolean

setup.precondition: voice_qa_report.gate_result = 'pass' for content_type 'post'; both platform approvals granted
setup.injected_fault: publish_x succeeds; publish_linkedin fails with a rate_limited error

steps: publisher calls publish_x, receives status 'published' with a post_url
publisher calls publish_linkedin independently, receives status 'failed' with error populated
publisher writes published_post.platform_status with BOTH entries populated with their true, independent states
publisher records event_type 'post_published' (since at least one platform succeeded) carrying the full platform_status object, not a summary boolean
assertions: no field anywhere on published_post collapses the two platform outcomes into a single boolean
the manager's fleet status roll-up, when asked about client_partial_005, names both platforms' states explicitly rather than reporting a generic 'published'
publisher does not auto-retry the LinkedIn publish itself -- a retry requires the bounded content-loop repair loop or a fresh human decision, not a silent internal retry

name: Pipeline isolation: a blocked site-build re-brand never pauses that same client's already-running content-loop

setup.precondition: client has a deployed site (ClientBrandMemory version 2) and an active, recurring content-loop already publishing successfully
setup.injected_fault: an explicit re-brand site-build pass is triggered and its voice_qa (content_type 'site') fails twice, exhausting SITE_MAX_RETRY_ATTEMPTS and blocking the site-build pipeline

steps: site-build re-brand pipeline for client_dual_006 exhausts SITE_MAX_RETRY_ATTEMPTS (2) and is marked 'blocked'
in the same cycle, client_dual_006's content-loop cadence tick fires independently, still reading the LAST GOOD ClientBrandMemory (version 2, not a half-written re-brand draft)
content_strategist, ghostwriter, voice_qa, and publisher complete a normal content-loop pass for client_dual_006 using version 2
assertions: content-loop's post_brief.based_on_memory_version == 2, never a half-written or drift-repair-pending version from the blocked re-brand pass
no fleet event from the blocked site-build pipeline references or blocks any content-loop event for the same client_id
the fleet status roll-up reports client_dual_006 in TWO separate buckets (site-build: blocked, content-loop: published) rather than one merged status
